error_quality: structured/free + JSON diagnostics with code/message/expected/actual/help/repair fields, e.g. "expected canonical declaration" actual "struct", and BLD004 "direct backend MVP does not support declarations other than functions"
notes: FINAL code guarantees balance>=0 via HAND-WRITTEN checks, not the type system. The account state is a plain i64 balance threaded through value-returning functions (the direct AArch64 backend MVP rejects record/struct local types and non-function top-level declarations, so an idiomatic Account struct + Result enum could not be built on this host target; the language accepts them: zero check reports languageOk=true but buildable=false). deposit/withdraw return the new balance, or the literal -1 as an error sentinel (safe because a valid balance is always >= 0). The apply() helper keeps the previous balance whenever a result is negative, so rejected operations leave the balance unchanged and it can never become negative. Negative amounts and zero are rejected by amount<=0; overlimit withdrawals by amount>bal. There is no compiler-verified contract on the invariant; correctness rests on the explicit guards plus the >=0/-1 sentinel discipline. Pass count is reported via the process exit code (return passed) because no stdout/println std helper could be recovered from diagnostics within the narrow bootstrap stdlib surface.
